question.label: Sharper feedback
question.name: Sharper feedback earlier — what are you usually waiting for before you ask?
question.description: Drills into his stated hesitation to surface the decision rule or caution behind delayed feedback-seeking.

answer: I have not built enough trust with adjacent teams yet.

assessment.note: He names low trust with adjacent teams as the reason he delays asking early, signaling mild misalignment risk and some drag from caution; [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'trust with adjacent teams' could be worth picking up next session.

new_queue.label: Gap awareness
new_queue.name: Where do you think you've fallen short of where you want to be?

new_queue.label: Questioning cadence
new_queue.name: What tends to stop you from asking a question early?

new_queue.label: Next signal
new_queue.name: What would you want me to see differently by our next check-in?

history.answer_excerpt: I probably should ask for sharper feedback earlier than I do.
I have not built enough trust with adjacent teams yet.
I think I am shipping, but I am not sure I am aiming at the highest-leverage work yet.
I have made a few calls independently, but I still over-index on being safe.

I can describe Principal impact in general, but this team's bar still feels fuzzy.
